programme.desc: A 1953 Marilyn Monroe pin-up poster; a diamond and platinum ring circa 1960; a 1910-1911 Philadelphia Athletics trophy, card and pin.

programme.title: A Chef's Life
programme.desc: A 92-year-old offers an entertaining tutorial in "beatin' peas."

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Catherine visits her first solo patient, who is experiencing persistent nausea; Nurse Crane, Trixie, Rosalind and Joyce participate in a women's liberation meeting.

programme.title: The Forsytes on Masterpiece
programme.desc: Chance encounters for cousins Joe and Soames Forsyte, rival heirs to the family firm, throw their steady lives into disarray.

programme.title: The Count of Monte Cristo on Masterpiece
programme.desc: After getting his ship safely home, Edmond Dantes is betrayed and sent to prison on a false charge.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.sub-title: What Is That Thing?; Buster's Best Behavior
programme.desc: A magical device solves problems in "What Is That Thing?"; Arthur and Buster trade places in "Buster's Best Behavior.".

programme.title: Odd Squad
programme.desc: The Mobile Unit is called to Pinehurst, N.C., to stop a villain who is stealing various objects.

programme.title: Wild Kratts
programme.desc: A miniaturized Chris gets stuck to a bee.

programme.title: Weather Hunters
programme.desc: A foggy Halloween almost derails Corky and Lily's trick-or-treating.

programme.title: Lyla in the Loop
programme.sub-title: Lyla's Lucky Strike; Lyla Out of the Loop
programme.desc: Lyla gets a strike her first time bowling but gets frustrated when she realizes bowling isn't as easy as she thought it was; Lyla feels left out when Everett and Ale's friendship thrives.

programme.title: Carl the Collector
programme.sub-title: Rhythm Blues; Giving Up the Ship
programme.desc: Lotta is frustrated learning a new dance, but realizes it is ok to experience music in her own way; Sheldon worries about telling Carl that he doesn't want to play pirates anymore.

programme.title: Daniel Tiger's Neighborhood
programme.desc: The whole neighborhood helps Dr. Anna and Baker Aker celebrate their wedding, including Daniel, who has an important job as ring bearer.

programme.title: Phoebe & Jay
programme.desc: Phoebe and Jay use a checklist to guide them through their tasks; Jay creates his first comic book and learns about the importance of page order.

programme.title: Sesame Street
programme.desc: Elmo's bike is great, but Abby's bike might be even better; Tamir teaches Elmo how to deal with feeling jealous and to enjoy the things Elmo already has.

programme.title: Work It Out Wombats!
programme.sub-title: Welcome to the Treeborhood; Ice Cream Bike
programme.desc: Devonte J. Squirrel moves to the Treeborhood; Devonte and the Wombats create a machine that makes ice cream using a bike.

programme.title: Donkey Hodie
programme.sub-title: Big Nest Bird Party; Pet Elephant Camp
programme.desc: Donkey tries to act like a bird at Duck Duck's bird party, but she does not enjoy herself; Grampy and Donkey miss Gregory while he is away at camp.

programme.title: Rosie's Rules
programme.sub-title: Iggy's Check Up; The New Park
programme.desc: Rosie helps Iggy get ready for his checkup; Rosie wants to play in the new park -- but it is a park for dogs.

programme.title: Elinor Wonders Why
programme.sub-title: Water You Doing?; Thinking About Blinking
programme.desc: Elinor and friends build a log bridge over a stream; the kids set out to learn everything they can about blinking when Ari is crowned the blinking champion at school.

programme.title: Alma's Way
programme.sub-title: Justice Sonia and Judge Alma; Justice Sonia and Umpire Alma
programme.desc: Inspired by Supreme Court Justice Sonia Sotomayor, Alma tries to help her friends determine what's fair; Justice Sotomayor encourages Alma to be an umpire at a kickball game.

programme.title: Molly of Denali
programme.sub-title: Turn on the Northern Lights; Fiddlesticks
programme.desc: Molly makes it her mission to show Trini the Northern Lights; Molly realizes she wants to play the drum instead of the fiddle, but has to figure out how to tell her friend, Oscar, that she won't be taking lessons from him anymore.

programme.title: Xavier Riddle and the Secret Museum
programme.sub-title: I Am Billie Jean King; I Am Arthur Ashe
programme.desc: Billie Jean King encourages Brad to take his first ballet class, even though he is worried about being the only boy in the class; Arthur Ashe helps Xavier, Yadina, and Brad see why they were wrong to exclude a new student at school from their game.

programme.title: Baking With Julia
programme.sub-title: Chocolate Mint Nightcaps With Marcel Desaulniers
programme.desc: Chef Marcel Desaulniers prepares oven-roasted plum cakes with chocolate sauce and chocolate-mint nightcaps.

programme.title: This Old House
programme.desc: Installing a warm roof; new framing poses plumbing challenges; rot is discovered on the original sill; exploring options to light the new ell.

programme.title: Wild Metropolis
programme.desc: Animals travel in and out of cities for food, shelter and to start a family.

programme.desc: The rise of politician and activist Bella Abzug and her commitment to women's rights and progressive causes.

programme.title: Rick Renner
programme.desc: Pilate did not want to crucify Jesus but he couldn't get out of it because it was God's will for Jesus to die for our sins.

programme.title: You'll Get Through This
programme.desc: Max Lucado discusses trusting God.

programme.title: Joyce Meyer Enjoying Everyday Life
programme.desc: Joyce Meyer, a Christian minister, discusses religious teachings and encourages people to live ethically.

programme.title: The Nicole Crank Show
programme.desc: Nicole Crank, the pastor of Faith Church in Missouri, talks about faith and everyday topics with her guests.

programme.title: Stakelbeck Tonight
programme.desc: Host Erick Stakelbeck takes viewers around the world; from Jerusalem to London to Washington, D.C.; pointing beyond with cutting-edge commentary, analysis and interviews focusing on ever-changing events and why they matter.

programme.title: Joel Osteen
programme.desc: Faith is what activates God's power; living in the knowledge that God is bigger than any problems; reaching out to God in faith.

programme.title: Main Stage
programme.desc: Charles Billingsley and TaRanda Greene; host Jason Crabb.

programme.title: Jesus: The Game Changer
programme.desc: Karl Faase travels to the U.K., U.S., Singapore, India and Australia, interviewing authors, academics and modern-day game changers about how the life and teaching of Jesus changed the world and why it matters.

programme.title: Insights -- Israel and the Middle East
programme.desc: Exploring the discoveries that support the Bible and help to understand the past.

programme.title: Signs & Secrets of the Messiah with Rabbi Jason Sobel
programme.sub-title: The Signs and Secrets of Healing (Part 2)
